answer:
**In "Subah Ka Sitara" (Morning Star, Bang-E-Dara Part 1, ch. 42), the central image is the morning star personified as a herald that forsakes the joys of sun and moon to announce dawn each day, despite its daily "death," yearning instead for an earthly existence—like a pearl, dew, or tear—that touches human love, sacrifice, and homeland.[^1][^2]** > The joy of sun and moon, I would forsake,
This herald's duty for the morning's sake.[^3] > True life is that which knows not Death's decree;
What life is this, where Death demands its fee?[^4] **In "Maah-e-Nau" (New Moon, Bang-E-Dara Part 1, ch. 30), the central image is the new moon as a faint, silent sliver—a "fragment" of the shattered sun "o’er the water’s face," an earring stolen from evening's bride or silver fish—waxing and waning mysteriously, evoking longing and restlessness without revealing its path.[^5][^6]** > The sun's great vessel, shattered, drowned in azure deep,
A single fragment o’er the water’s face does sweep.[^7] Dawn promises full renewal and "eternal life" through awakening light, human connection, and the "fire of love to all mankind"—a decisive end to night's void that the new moon, transient and suffocating in its dark emergence, cannot deliver.[^8][^9] [^1]: Allama Iqbal, *Bang-E-Dara Part 1*, ch. 42 "Subah Ka Sitara" ([/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-1-55b25c/42-subah-ka-sitara/translation/](/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-1-55b25c/42-subah-ka-sitara/translation/)). [^2]: Ibid., [p-2aceb1](/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-1-55b25c/42-subah-ka-sitara/translation/#p-2aceb1). [^3]: Ibid., [p-aab796](/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-1-55b25c/42-subah-ka-sitara/translation/#p-aab796). [^4]: Ibid., [p-e76893](/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-1-55b25c/42-subah-ka-sitara/translation/#p-e76893). [^5]: Allama Iqbal, *Bang-E-Dara Part 1*, ch. 30 "Maah-e-Nau" ([/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-1-55b25c/30-maah-e-nau/translation/](/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-1-55b25c/30-maah-e-nau/translation/)). [^6]: Ibid., [p-04dbb8](/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-1-55b25c/30-maah-e-nau/translation/#p-04dbb8). [^7]: Ibid., [p-85f443](/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-1-55b25c/30-maah-e-nau/translation/#p-85f443). [^8]: *Subah Ka Sitara*, [p-2aceb1](/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-1-55b25c/42-subah-ka-sitara/translation/#p-2aceb1). [^9]: *Maah-e-Nau*, [p-04dbb8](/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-1-55b25c/30-maah-e-nau/translation/#p-04dbb8).
